popularStory.headline: Joanna Isaac
popularStory.description:
Biography
Joanna was the daughter of Thomas de Isaac and Matilda Bruce . Robert I, king of Scots, was her maternal grandfather, and David II, king of Scots, was her uncle.

Historians have speculated …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Joanna Jonet Isaac de Ergadia於1337年出生在英國蘇格蘭費伏登福姆林時，她的父親Thomas de Isaac31歲，母親Princess Matilda Maud Bruce34歲。她於1362年與Eóghan John Macdougall在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1399年11月8日在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾享年62歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭斯林史爾坎布斯肯尼斯 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joanna Jonet Isaac de Ergadia于1337年出生在英国苏格兰費伏登福姆林时，她的父亲Thomas de Isaac31岁，母亲Princess Matilda Maud Bruce34岁。她于1362年与Eóghan John Macdougall在英国苏格兰阿吉爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1399年11月8日在英国苏格兰阿吉爾享年62岁，埋葬在苏格兰斯林史爾坎布斯肯尼斯 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Isaac
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish, English, Welsh, and French: from the Biblical Hebrew personal name Yishaq ‘he laughs’. This was the name of the son of Abraham (Genesis 21:3) by his wife Sarah. The traditional explanation of the name is that Abraham and Sarah laughed with joy at the birth of a son to them in their old age, but a more plausible explanation is that the name originally meant ‘may God laugh’, i.e. ‘smile on him’. Like Abraham , this name has always been immensely popular among Jews, but was also widely used in medieval Europe among Christians. Hence it is the surname of many gentile families as well as Jews. In England and Wales it was one of the Old Testament names that were particularly popular among Nonconformists in the 17th–19th centuries, which accounts for its frequency as a Welsh surname. (Welsh surnames were generally formed much later than English ones.) In eastern Europe the personal name in its various vernacular forms was popular in Orthodox (Russian, Ukrainian, and Bulgarian), Catholic (Polish), and Protestant (Czech) Churches. It was borne by a 5th-century father of the Armenian Church and by a Spanish martyr executed by the Moorish rulers of Cordoba in AD 851 on account of his tireless polemics against Islam. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Iskhaq or Ishak , Hungarian Izsák, and Slovenian Izak, and also their patronymics, e.g. Serbian Isakov . The name Isaac is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Issac .

variants.name: Abraham
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Mathai, Mohan, Raju, Saji, Prasad, Anil, Babu, Biju, Leela, Sajan, Shibu, Suma.

English, French, Spanish, German, Slovenian, Ethiopian, and Assyrian/Chaldean; Hungarian (Ábrahám), Slovak (mainly Abrahám, also Ábrahám), Czech (also Abrahám): from the Hebrew personal name ʾAbraham (modern spelling Avraham), borne by the Biblical patriarch revered by Jews as the founding father of the Jewish people (Genesis 11–25) and by Muslims as founder of all the Semitic peoples, both Hebrew and Arab (compare Ibrahim ). The original name of the Biblical patriarch was probably Abram , meaning ‘high father’ (from ab father, ram ‘exalted’), while its later form is explained in Genesis 17:5 as being derived from Hebrew ab hamon goyim ‘father of a multitude of nations’. It was widely used as a personal name among Christians as well as Jews in the Middle Ages. The name Abraham is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. As a surname of Hungarian origin Abraham is also found in Romania. — Note: Since Ethiopians do not have hereditary surnames, the Ethiopian name was registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as an equivalent of Gaelic Mac an Bhreitheamhan ‘son of the judge’. See McBroom and compare Breheny .

variants.name: Isakson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Alf, Knut.

Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish Isaksson: patronymic from the personal name Isak (see Isaac ). This is also an Americanized form of the Norwegian and Danish cognate Isaksen . Compare Isackson .

variants.name: Ishaq
meanings: Arabic and Assyrian/Chaldean: from the personal name ’Isḥāq, Arabic and Syriac form of the Biblical name Isaac , which is also the name of a prophet in Islam (see the Koran 37:112). This form of the name is most common among Muslims in the Indian subcontinent and Nigeria. Among Assyrians/Chaldeans the name is also commonly spelled Iskhaq. Compare Ishak .

variants.name: Isaksen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Nils, Borg, Gunner.
Norwegian and Danish: patronymic from the personal name Isak (see Isaac ). Compare Isackson and Isakson .

variants.name: Isak
meanings: Jewish: variant of   Isaac  .
German: variant of   Isaak  .

variants.name: Isaacson
meanings: Jewish and English: patronymic from the Old Testament personal name Isaac + -son. In North America, this surname has probably also assimilated cognate names from other languages.

variants.name: Iskhakov
meanings: Muslim (territory of the former USSR) and Jewish (from Uzbekistan): patronymic from the personal name Iskhak, equivalent to Isaac , with the Russian possessive suffix -ov.

variants.name: Isaacs
meanings: Jewish, English, and Dutch: patronymic from the Old Testament personal name   Isaac  . This surname is also established in South Africa. It is rare in the Netherlands.

variants.name: Isaak
meanings: Jewish: variant of   Isaac  .
German: from the personal name Isaak, German form of Isaac .

variants.name: Isakov
meanings: Muslim, Jewish (from the territory of the former USSR), Russian, Serbian, and Bulgarian: patronymic from the personal name Isak (see Isaac ), with the Slavic possessive suffix -ov.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Joanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

variants.name: Joannah
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. nameMeanings.name: Jonet
meanings: A Scottish form of Janet.

en.lifeSummary: When Joanna Jonet Isaac de Ergadia was born in 1337, in Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas de Isaac, was 31 and her mother, Princess Matilda Maud Bruce, was 34. She married Eóghan John Macdougall in 1362, in Argyll, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died on 8 November 1399, in Argyll, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 62, and was buried in Cambuskenneth Abbey, Stirlingshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joanna Jonet Isaac d’Ergadia est née en 1337, à Dunfermline, Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas de Isaac, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Princess Matilda Maud Bruce, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Eóghan John Macdougall en 1362, à Argyll, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 novembre 1399, à Argyll, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Cambuskenneth Abbey, Stirlingshire, Écosse.

ja.lifeSummary: Joanna Jonet Isaac de Ergadiaが1337年にイギリススコットランドフィフダンフエルムラインで生まれた時，父Thomas de Isaacは31歳，母Princess Matilda Maud Bruceは34歳であった。1362年にイギリススコットランドアルグイルでEóghan John Macdougallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1399年11月8日にイギリススコットランドアルグイルで62歳で死去し，スコットランドスティルリングシュアイアカンブスケネス アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joanna Jonet Isaac de Ergadia(이)가 1337년 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 던퍼라이인에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas de Isaac(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Princess Matilda Maud Bruce(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1362년 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이에서 Eóghan John Macdougall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1399년 11월 8일 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 스티를링실르 캄버스켄스 애브브이에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joanna Jonet Isaac de Ergadia родилась в 1337 г. в местности дунфирмлаин, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas de Isaac было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Princess Matilda Maud Bruce, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Eóghan John Macdougall в 1362 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла 8 ноября 1399 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности камбускеннет эбби, стирлингшир, Шотландия.

Se casó con Eóghan John Macdougall en 1362, en Argyll, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 8 de noviembre de 1399, en Argyll, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultada en Cambuskenneth Abbey, Stirlingshire, Scotland.
